regions.us-ports: Today at Miami, ENE winds at 10 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability support normal embarkation and shore excursion operations, though light drizzle is possible. Heat Advisory in effect for Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 86°F) and San Diego (temperature up to 90°F). Over the next 48 hours, ESE winds increase to 16 knots with a 21% rain chance on Day 2, followed by SE winds at 11 knots and a 25% rain probability on Day 3 as thunderstorms develop, which may affect outdoor activities and port scheduling for vessels operating in the region.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today east-northeast winds at 14 knots with clear skies and 12% rain probability support normal embarkation and shore excursion operations across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands, though isolated drizzle may occur. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, southeast winds increase to 19 knots with a 22% rain probability that may affect water-based activities in Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth. Beyond 48 hours, east-southeast winds ease to 12 knots but thunderstorms are forecast with a 24% rain probability that could affect port operations and outdoor excursions in Puerto Plata, La Romana, Santo Domingo, and Samaná.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today easterly winds of 14 knots with partly cloudy skies and 16% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursions across San Juan, St. Thomas, St. Croix, St. Maarten, St. Kitts, and Antigua. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds increase slightly to 15 knots with less than 10% rain probability on Day 2, maintaining favorable embarkation and disembarkation conditions throughout the region. Beyond 48 hours, Day 3 shows easterly winds holding at 15 knots with a 26% rain probability, which may affect afternoon shore activities but poses no significant constraint to cruise operations.
regions.western-caribbean: Today across the Western Caribbean, clear skies and ENE winds at 8 knots with less than 10% rain probability support normal port operations and shore excursions in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City, though heat index values are reaching 100 degrees, requiring passengers to take precautions during outdoor activities. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, ENE winds remain at 8 knots while a 28% rain probability brings scattered showers that may affect Cozumel and Costa Maya on Day 2, though port calls should proceed with minor delays possible.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today easterly winds reach 20 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, creating manageable conditions for port operations and most shore excursions across Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena despite the brisk wind. Through the next 48 hours, easterly winds hold steady at 19-20 knots with occasional drizzle and less than 10% rain probability at all four ports, presenting no significant operational constraints for vessel movement or passenger activities. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds gradually ease to 17 knots by day three with drizzle persisting and less than 10% rain probability, indicating a stabilizing pattern with minimal weather impact to cruise operations throughout the region.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today easterly winds at 13 knots with drizzle and 13% rain probability will allow port operations and shore excursions in Barbados, Antigua, and St. Maarten to proceed normally with minimal weather delays. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, easterly winds at 12 knots combined with rain showers and 18% rain probability may affect excursion timing in Grenada, St. Vincent, and Dominica, though cruise operations will remain largely unaffected. By day three, a 43% rain probability with drizzle and 13-knot easterly winds is expected to affect outdoor activities across St. Lucia, Martinique, and St. Kitts, requiring flexible scheduling for tender operations and beach-based shore excursions.
regions.eastern-pacific: Today excessive heat with a heat index reaching 108F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Cabo San Lucas, Ensenada, Huatulco, Manzanillo, Mazatlan, and Puerto Vallarta, with westerly winds at 8 knots and overcast skies limiting relief. Day 2 brings continued excessive heat with a heat index reaching 109F alongside westerly winds at 10 knots and overcast conditions with less than 10% rain probability, affecting all ports similarly. By day 3, elevated heat with a heat index reaching 103F and westerly winds at 10 knots with 20% rain probability may affect shore excursions, though conditions gradually moderate across the region.
regions.los-angeles: Today light WSW winds of 12 knots and clear skies support normal embarkation and disembarkation operations across Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, with less than 10% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, WSW winds ease to 10 knots on Day 2 as overcast skies develop, but less than 10% rain probability keeps all ports operationally clear for passenger movement and shore excursions. By Day 3, southerly winds lighten to 7 knots with partly cloudy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise operations across the region through the forecast period.
regions.ensenada: Today light westerly winds at 1 knot will support clear skies and smooth conditions across Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos, with less than 10% rain probability allowing unobstructed port operations and shore excursion access throughout the day. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly-southwest winds will increase to 8-9 knots with fog developing at all three ports, reducing visibility but maintaining less than 10% rain probability and permitting continued embarkation and disembarkation activities. Beyond 48 hours, fog will persist with westerly-southwest winds at 8 knots and drizzle developing on day three, though less than 10% rain probability indicates minimal operational disruption to cruise schedules.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today excessive heat with a heat index reaching 108 degrees poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions in Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, with westerly winds at 8 knots and overcast skies limiting natural cooling. Tomorrow the heat index climbs to 109 degrees under continued westerly 10-knot winds and overcast conditions across all three ports, with less than 10% rain probability offering no relief from the dangerous thermal stress. By day three, westerly 10-knot winds persist with overcast skies and a 20% rain probability that may affect port operations, though the heat index moderates slightly to 103 degrees.
regions.mazatlan: Today elevated heat with heat index reaching 104F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, with thunderstorms and 40% rain probability under light SSW winds at 8 knots limiting embarkation and disembarkation delays. Day 2 through Day 3 will see strengthening thunderstorm activity with 55% rain probability expected to affect port operations on Day 2 and 61% rain probability expected to impact all three ports on Day 3 as SE winds increase to 10 knots, requiring extended mooring time and potential tender service modifications.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today elevated heat with a heat index reaching 104F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, with WSW winds at 7 knots and thunderstorms that may affect port operations and embarkation schedules given the 40% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, heat index values reach 102F while NNE winds at 5 knots accompany a 49% rain probability and continued thunderstorm activity that may affect all four ports.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today light southwest winds at 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursions throughout the region, including embarkation at Naples, Civitavecchia, and Genoa. Over the next 24-48 hours, northeast winds increase to 12 knots with partly cloudy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for passengers at all Central Mediterranean ports from Marseille through Dubrovnik. Beyond 48 hours, winds shift to the east-southeast at 8 knots as skies become overcast with less than 10% rain probability, presenting no operational constraints for cruise operations or excursion planning through day three.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today northeast winds at 9 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursions across Athens, Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, Corfu, Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria. Tomorrow south-southeast winds at 8 knots may affect outdoor activities as 23% rain probability brings scattered showers to the region, particularly impacting afternoon excursions. By day three, north-northwest winds at 10 knots with overcast conditions and less than 10% rain probability will restore stable conditions for vessel transits and passenger operations.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today rain showers with an 80% rain probability are expected to affect port operations and shore excursions in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway, with SE winds at 4 kt keeping conditions relatively calm despite persistent precipitation. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, SE winds will increase slightly to 5 kt while rain showers and a 74% rain probability continue to impact all three ports through Day 2. By Day 3, northerly winds at 5 kt and an overcast sky with 17% rain probability will mark a transition toward drier conditions across the region.
